english: kyoṅ (kyong), also kyoṅ-bu (kyong bu), small shovel, scraper Sch.
kyom (kyom), also kyóm-kyom (kyom kyom), 1. flexible, but without elasticity, flabby, loose, lax. — 2. also kyom-kyóm (kyom kyom), of irregular shape, not rectilinear.
kyor (kyor), also kyor-kyór (kyor kyor) weak, feeble, unfortified Cs.
kyol (kyol), also kyol-kyól (kyol kyol) = kyor (kyor) Cs.
krag (krag) v. bkrag (bkrag).
kraṅ-ṅé (krang nge) standing, kraṅ sdod-pa (krang sdod pa) to stand Zam. cf. kroṅ (krong).

krád-pa (krad pa) leather half-boot or shoe, as it is worn by the lower class of people, often with a woolen leg; krad-rgyún (krad rgyun) Cs. a long narrow piece of leather to fasten the sole to the upper-leather; *tad-kyi* (*tad kyi*) W. *ta'-kyi* (*ta' kyi*) C. (or gyi (gyi), from gyi-na? (gyi na?)) a worn-out leather sole.
krab-kráb (krab krab) v. krab-pa (krab pa).
kram (kram) W. cabbage, kram-mṅár (kram mngar) sweet or fresh cabbage; kram-skyúr (kram skyur) sour or macerated cabbage Cs. (?)
kṛi-kṛi (kri kri) n. of a fabulous king of India Glr., not mentioned in the Ssk. dictionaries.
kṛiṣṇa-sā-ra (k+Ri Sh+Na sa ra) Ssk. the spotted antelope Pth.
kru-krú (kru kru) W. windpipe. *ṭu-ṭú dam-te śi-će* (*tu tu dam te shi ce*) to be strangled.
kre-nág (kre nag) smut of a kettle Sch. (= sre-nag? (sre nag?))

kroṅ-króṅ (krong krong) standing upright, e. g. books (opp. to *gyél-kaṅ* (*gyel kang*) laid down, lying W.); when used of persons it means also: standing on one's knees, kneeling in an upright position.
kron-krón (kron kron) hanging, *ton-ton-la dug-će* (*ton ton la dug ce*) to hang, to be suspended in the air W.
kla-klo (kla klo) 1. Ssk. म्लेच्छ barbarian. — 2. in later times: Moslem, Mahometan; Mahometanism. Was.
klag-čor (klag cor) clamour, noise Cs.

klad (klad), acc. to Liś. (lish.) = goṅ (gong) what is above; hence klád-pa (klad pa), also glad (glad), 1. head. 2. brain, and klad-ma (klad ma) beginning, top Sch.; gur-klád (gur klad) chimney of a felt-tent. — klad-kor (klad kor) v. kor (kor). — klad-rgyá (klad rgya) the skin covering the brain, pia mater; klad-rgyas (klad rgyas), = lhá-ba (lha ba), 'the bloody marrow in the bones' Sch., or simply 'brain' Schf. — klad-sgo (klad sgo) the fontanel in the infant cranium Sch. — klad-čuṅ (klad chung) the cerebellum Sch. — klad-gźuṅ (klad gzhung) spinal marrow. — klad-gźér (klad gzher) headache Med. — klad-śúbs (klad shubs) = klad-rgyá (klad rgya) Sch.
klan-ka (klan ka) 1. censure, blame Cs., klan byéd-pa (klan byed pa), ₒdebs-pa ('debs pa) to blame, cf. skur-klán (skur klan). — 2. klan ₒtsol-ba (klan 'tsol ba) to seek brawls Pth.
klán-pa (klan pa) v. klon-pa (klon pa).

klu (klu), Ssk. नाग, originally: hooded snake, cobra di capello; in this specific sense, however, it is never used in Tibetan, whereas every child knows and believes in the mythological signification: serpent-demon, a demigod with a human head and the body of a serpent, living in fountains, rivers etc., commanding over great treasures, causing rain and certain maladies, and becoming dangerous when in anger: gdúg-pa (gdug pa) is therefore a usual epitheton of such demons. klui skad (klu'i skad) means the Prakrit language, klui yi-ge (klu'i yi ge) the Nagari character of Ssk. letters, viz. that which is called varttula, in contrast to the holy landza (lan dza), lhai yi-ge (lha'i yi ge). — klui gnod-pa (klu'i gnod pa) or skyon (skyon) diseases of unknown origin. — klu-mo (klu mo) a female serpent-demon. — klu-sgrúb (klu sgrub), prop. n., Nagarjuna, a famous Buddhist divine. — klu-mdúd (klu mdud) Codonopsis ovata. — klu-nad (klu nad) = klui-gnod-pa (klu'i gnod pa). — klu-phrúg (klu phrug) a young Lu. — klu-smán (klu sman) 'n. of a medicine' Cs., but sman (sman) and klu-smán (klu sman) are also synonyms for klu (klu), Glr., Mil. etc.
kluṅ (klung) river, more com. ču-klúṅ (chu klung), B.
kluṅs (klungs) 1. cultivated land, field, klúṅs-su skye (klungs su skye) it grows on cultivated ground Wdn. — 2. a complex of fields, dkar- (dkar-)
